summary: Tight Meadowlands set with stellar Scarlet>Fire and rare Walking The Dog
blurb: The show delivers a punchy first set highlighted by Walkin' The Dog into Deal and tight renditions of Bird Song and Looks Like Rain. The second set shines with the classic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, an inspired Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World, and a powerful Throwing Stones close.
review: On November 11, 1985 at Meadowlands Arena the Dead turned in a high‑energy performance that feels like a celebratory finale to their East Coast fall run. Jerry’s voice is surprisingly strong for ’85 and his guitar work stays "packed and impressive" despite a minor lyric slip on Althea; Phil’s bass thunders with the trademark deep “bombs” that many reviewers called solid and rumbling, anchoring the band’s groove. Bob Weir rides high, loud and clearly having a blast, adding extra spark to every song. Brent’s keyboards sit comfortably in the mix, while Mickey’s drumming occasionally wobbles on an intro but Bill swoops in to keep the pulse steady. The set is peppered with rare gems – a Walking The Dog → Deal opener that fires up the crowd, a flawless Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition, and an exhilarating Throwing Stones that caps an otherwise textbook second‑set flow. With Dan Healy’s crisp SBD capture (the recommended recording) you get crystal‑clear instrument separation, making this show a must‑listen for anyone craving classic mid‑80s Dead magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six out of seven analyses are explicitly positive, praising the tight playing, standout songs (Scarlet>Fire, Walking The Dog, Throwing Stones) and overall energy; only one mixed review notes a minor Althea glitch. Consistent high praise across recordings yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Dan Healy’s soundboard mix is crystal‑clear, well‑balanced and captures the entire show with only a tiny fade‑out at the end.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue shines as a classic peak
Rare Walking The Dog opening that leads into Deal sets an immediate party vibe
Throwing Stones delivers a high‑octane jam and closes the second set powerfully
Phil’s bass bombs provide deep, rumbling foundation throughout
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: Lead work stays packed and impressive; sweet licks and a strong ’85 voice despite a minor lyric slip on Althea.
band_performance.Phil: Bass bombs are prominent, deep and rumbling, providing powerful low‑end drive.
band_performance.Bob: Loud, energetic and clearly enjoying himself, adding extra spark to the performance.
band_performance.Mickey: Slightly off‑tempo on an intro in one mix but otherwise drives the groove with primal beats.
band_performance.Billy: Steps in when needed, keeping the groove solid and saving any timing slips.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
